requirements.title: Load and validate execution profile configuration
requirements.summary: Spark must load $SPARK_HOME/config/execution-profiles.toml into typed worker and profile models, synthesize a built-in native default only when the file is missing, and reject invalid selected profile graphs before launch.
acceptance_criteria: Given no execution-profiles.toml and no selected configured profile, Spark resolves a native-compatible default profile.
Given valid TOML containing workers and native, local_container, and remote_worker profiles, the loader returns normalized models with defaults applied.
Given invalid worker or profile fields, validation errors identify the failing worker/profile id and field.
Given a remote_worker profile, validation requires an existing enabled worker, image, control_project_root, worker_project_root, and worker_runtime_root.
Given a local_container profile without image, validation fails before launch.
Given a native profile, image, worker, and root mapping fields are not required.
Given a selected invalid profile, Spark does not silently fall back to another profile.
requirements.notes: Use the same runtime home resolution path as the existing server settings. Missing config is valid only for synthesized native default behavior.

requirements.title: Resolve execution profiles by durable profile id
requirements.summary: Run launch must select by execution_profile_id using explicit override, project default, Spark default, then implementation default, while rejecting disabled or missing selections before execution.
acceptance_criteria: Given an explicit run execution_profile_id, it takes precedence over project and Spark defaults.
Given no run override and a project default execution_profile_id, the project default is selected.
Given no run override or project default and a Spark default execution_profile_id, the Spark default is selected.
Given no configured selection, Spark preserves existing native launch behavior.
Given a disabled profile or a profile referencing a disabled worker, launch fails before contacting a worker.
Given a missing selected profile id, launch fails before execution.
Public launch payloads, CLI commands, workspace API payloads, and chat-generated run requests use execution_profile_id as the durable selection field instead of execution_container_image.
requirements.notes: The old direct image selection path should be removed as a public contract rather than kept as a parallel launch mechanism.

requirements.title: Map project roots without source preparation
requirements.summary: Remote-worker launches must translate the control-plane project path to a worker path by path-segment-aware root replacement and must never clone, sync, upload, repair, or mutate project source implicitly.
acceptance_criteria: Given a control project path under control_project_root, Spark computes the worker path by preserving the relative suffix under worker_project_root.
Given a control project path outside control_project_root, launch fails before contacting a worker.
Path matching is path-segment aware and does not accept raw string prefix false positives.
Path translation does not create directories, copy files, infer git metadata, or run setup commands.
The mapped worker project path and worker runtime root are persisted in launch metadata and passed to the worker.
The execution container path values exposed to node execution match the worker-side paths persisted in run metadata.
requirements.notes: Worker-local existence validation is covered by worker admission/preparation requirements.

requirements.title: Authenticate and validate worker compatibility
requirements.summary: The control plane must authenticate worker calls with bearer tokens sourced from auth_token_env and must validate worker health/protocol compatibility under the chosen launch policy.
acceptance_criteria: Given a remote worker profile, the control plane reads the bearer token from the worker auth_token_env before authenticated calls.
Given a missing or empty auth token environment variable, launch fails before contacting authenticated worker endpoints.
Every control-plane worker request sends Authorization: Bearer <token>.
The worker rejects missing, malformed, or invalid tokens with 401 or 403.
The control plane rejects workers that do not advertise a compatible v1 protocol version.
Worker health and worker-info responses are available for settings/debug validation without becoming profile selection inputs.
requirements.notes: Bearer-token auth is sufficient for v1; mutual TLS is out of scope for this requirement.

requirements.title: Implement worker run lifecycle API
requirements.summary: A standalone worker FastAPI app must expose v1 run admission, run snapshot, cancellation, and cleanup endpoints with specified idempotency, validation, and structured error behavior.
acceptance_criteria: spark-server worker serve starts a standalone worker FastAPI app rather than mounting the worker API into the Spark control-plane app.
POST /v1/runs validates request shape, authorization, protocol compatibility, image policy, path policy, capability compatibility, immediate resource admission, and idempotency compatibility.
POST /v1/runs returns 202 Accepted with run_id, worker_id, status, event_url, and last_sequence for admitted asynchronous preparation.
Repeated POST /v1/runs with compatible inputs is idempotent; conflicting inputs are rejected.
GET /v1/runs/{run_id} returns worker-side status, active node, container handle, image, mapped path, last sequence, worker snapshot, and last error when present.
POST /v1/runs/{run_id}/cancel emits cancellation state, stops accepting new node execution, and signals active execution best-effort.
DELETE /v1/runs/{run_id} idempotently removes run-scoped container/runtime state or returns a non-error empty state when already removed.
All JSON error responses use the specified error.code, error.message, error.retryable, and error.details shape.
requirements.notes: The worker service is shipped in the same Python distribution as Spark.

requirements.title: Bridge worker node execution to run-node process protocol
requirements.summary: For each accepted node execution, the worker must run spark-server worker run-node inside the run-scoped execution container and translate process messages into remote events and final node outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: The worker rejects node execution before run_ready.
POST /v1/runs/{run_id}/nodes accepts one v1 active node execution per worker-side run unless a future profile explicitly supports parallel execution.
Repeated node requests with the same node_execution_id and compatible payload are idempotent.
Repeated node requests with the same node_execution_id and conflicting payload return 409 Conflict.
The worker writes the node execution request to run-node stdin and reads process stdout messages.
Process event messages are converted into SSE events with required run and node metadata.
Process human-gate, child-run, and child-status requests are converted into corresponding SSE request events.
A valid serialized Outcome, context updates, and runtime metadata are delivered through node_result.
Process crashes, invalid process messages, or missing final results are converted into node_failed.
requirements.notes: The current local-container worker process protocol is the compatibility boundary; Docker lifecycle remains worker-owned.

requirements.title: Provide ordered worker SSE event streams
requirements.summary: Workers must stream required run, node, gate, child-run, cancellation, cleanup, and log events over SSE with monotonic per-run sequence ids and reconnect support.
acceptance_criteria: GET /v1/runs/{run_id}/events requires authentication and never accepts unauthenticated SSE streams.
Every SSE event uses the sequence number as the SSE id and the worker event type as the SSE event field.
Every event includes run id, sequence number, event type, timestamp, worker id, execution profile id, and payload.
Node-related events include node id and node attempt when available.
The worker emits run_ready before accepting node execution.
If preparation fails, the worker emits run_failed with a stable code, message, retryable flag, and optional details before any node executes.
Reconnect supports Last-Event-ID and ?after=<sequence>.
The required event types listed in the spec can be emitted and serialized.
requirements.notes: SSE sequence handling is both a UI continuity and correctness requirement.

requirements.title: Execute remote-worker runs from the control plane
requirements.summary: For remote_worker profiles, the Spark control plane must use HTTP/SSE transport to admit runs, wait for run_ready, submit nodes, apply node outcomes to canonical run state, and fail unrecoverable event gaps.
acceptance_criteria: Remote-worker run launch resolves and validates the selected profile and worker before worker admission.
Launch persists launch-time execution metadata before or during run admission so later config edits do not rewrite historical metadata.
The control plane calls POST /v1/runs and opens or resumes the run event stream.
The control plane does not submit any node before receiving run_ready.
If run_failed arrives before run_ready, launch/preparation fails and no node result is applied.
Each control-plane node execution is submitted with POST /v1/runs/{run_id}/nodes.
node_result events are applied to canonical Attractor run state using the existing Outcome contract.
node_failed, worker run_failed, callback delivery exhaustion, and unrecoverable SSE gaps record active run failure.
Duplicate SSE events are ignored by sequence id.
Missing/gapped SSE events fail the run unless GET /v1/runs/{run_id} proves no required run, node, gate, child-run, or terminal event was lost.
Worker events and logs are surfaced into the normal run event history.
requirements.notes: The worker never decides graph routing; the control plane remains authoritative after each node result.

requirements.title: Forward human-gate and child-run callbacks
requirements.summary: Remote execution must keep human-gate, child-run, and child-status authority in Spark while forwarding requested answers and results back to the worker through idempotent callback endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: When the worker emits human_gate_request, the control plane records and surfaces the gate through existing Spark UI/API paths.
When an operator answers a gate, the control plane calls /v1/runs/{run_id}/human-gates/{gate_id}/answer.
Human-gate answer delivery is idempotent by gate_id and rejects conflicting repeated answers with 409 Conflict.
When the worker emits child_run_request, the control plane launches and records the authoritative child run and posts the result to /v1/runs/{run_id}/child-runs/{request_id}/result.
When the worker emits child_status_request, the control plane reads authoritative child-run state and posts the result to /v1/runs/{run_id}/child-status/{request_id}/result.
Child-run and child-status result deliveries are idempotent by request_id and reject conflicting repeated results with 409 Conflict.
Callback delivery failure after configured retries records active run failure.
requirements.notes: The worker only receives callback data needed to let the run-node process continue.

requirements.title: Persist execution metadata snapshots
requirements.summary: Run records and seeded run context must capture launch-time execution placement metadata for native, local_container, and remote_worker runs.
acceptance_criteria: Run records persist execution_mode and execution_profile_id for all runs.

Remote-worker run records persist execution_worker_id, execution container image snapshot, mapped worker project path, worker version snapshot, worker capability snapshot, and profile capability snapshot.
Local-container and remote-worker records persist the resolved profile image as an execution container image snapshot, not as a selection input.
Run context includes the same execution metadata keys needed for observability.
Later edits to execution-profiles.toml do not mutate historical run metadata.
Run details can expose cleanup failures in logs/details without silently rewriting a terminal workflow outcome unless an explicit runtime policy says otherwise.
requirements.notes: Existing execution_container_image metadata can remain as snapshot metadata where compatible, but it must not be the durable selection field.

requirements.title: Expose execution workers and profiles in UI/API surfaces
requirements.summary: Settings, project settings, flow launch, and run details must surface execution worker/profile observability and selection as execution placement rather than a separate workflow type.
acceptance_criteria: Settings exposes an Execution Workers section containing configured workers, configured profiles, worker health, worker status, capabilities, version, enabled state, and validation errors.
Project settings exposes a default execution profile selector.
Flow launch UI exposes the effective project default, optional run override selector, selected mode, selected profile label/id, and validation state before launch.
Run details expose execution mode, execution profile id, worker id, worker URL label, image, mapped worker path, worker version snapshot, and capabilities snapshot.
Worker health and worker-info are consumed for Settings visibility without allowing those endpoints to select profiles.
Remote execution appears as an execution placement choice and not as a different workflow type.
requirements.notes: Completed in M4 with observable Settings, workspace/project defaults, launch, run-detail, CLI, chat, and frontend contract coverage.

requirements.title: Keep Flow DOT non-authoritative for worker selection
requirements.summary: Flow DOT must not select remote workers or override disabled/unavailable profile constraints in v1; any future DOT capability hints are non-binding hints only.
acceptance_criteria: DOT-authored content cannot select a worker directly.
DOT-authored hints cannot override a disabled profile.
DOT-authored hints cannot make an unavailable root mapping valid.
Spark resolves the actual execution profile only from project and run launch state.
Existing DOT parsing and validation behavior remains compatible with workflows that do not mention execution placement.
requirements.notes: Completed in M4 with DOT non-authority guardrails validated through DSL and API/frontend contract coverage.

requirements.title: Place remote execution under Attractor execution runtime
requirements.summary: Remote execution implementation must live under the Attractor execution runtime package with thin integration points in server, workspace, app, and CLI modules.
acceptance_criteria: Execution profile models, loading, validation, metadata seeding, transports, request/response/event models, worker app/router, and worker run lifecycle are owned under src/attractor/execution/.
src/attractor/api/server.py remains a thin dispatcher that resolves selected execution profile and dispatches to native, local_container, or remote_worker execution.
src/attractor/handlers/execution_container.py may remain compatibility glue but does not become the long-term remote execution package.
src/spark/workspace/ owns project defaults, launch payloads, and workspace API exposure for execution profile selection.
src/spark/app.py and src/spark/workspace/api.py own UI-facing routes and settings surfaces.
src/spark/server_cli.py owns spark-server worker serve and worker run-node entrypoints.
The worker API is not mounted into the normal Spark control-plane app.
requirements.notes: The package ships in the same Python distribution as Spark; no separate installable package is required.

requirements.title: Record failure and cleanup semantics
requirements.summary: Launch, admission, preparation, active run, cancellation, cleanup, and orphan cleanup failures must be reported at the correct boundary with observable state.
acceptance_criteria: Launch fails before contacting a worker for missing/disabled profiles, disabled/missing workers, unmappable roots, missing auth tokens, and blocking health/info validation.

Run admission fails before asynchronous preparation for rejected tokens, unsupported protocol, malformed requests, forbidden images, incompatible capabilities, immediate resource unavailability, and conflicting repeated run admission.

Worker preparation emits run_failed before node execution for missing mapped paths, unavailable mounts/devices/resources, container creation/reuse failure, and image pull/availability failure after admission.
Active run failure is recorded for unrecoverable SSE gaps, worker run_failed, node_failed, and callback delivery retry exhaustion.
Cancellation interrupts active worker execution, stops new node submission, emits run_canceling and run_canceled when complete, and leaves cleanup available.
Cleanup failures are visible in run details/logs without silently rewriting terminal workflow outcome unless explicitly configured.
Workers provide an orphan cleanup policy for admitted runs whose control plane disappears, and cleanup is visible through worker logs and run snapshots when observable.
requirements.notes: Failure boundary tests should assert observable launch/API/run-event behavior rather than source text.
